note: legitimate shared decl: Universal property is the general category-theoretic pattern; IsLimit (structure IsLimit (t : Cone F), Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/IsLimit.lean:55, docstring 'each cone on F admits a unique cone morphism to t') is precisely stated as a universal-property definition -- exact match, no field/object issue (Q1417809 classes P279 Q937228/Q5399590 are concept types, not a discipline)

legitimate shared decl: Q1322614 'limit' (category theory term, P31 Q24034552) is the object IsLimit formalizes (a cone t is a limit cone iff IsLimit t) -- exact match, legitimate multi-to-multi alongside Q1417809 since limit and its universal-property characterization are the same mathematical content viewed two ways

legitimate shared decl: Q180907 'bijection' (mathematical function which is a one-to-one mapping of sets) is exactly def Bijective (f : α → β) := Injective f ∧ Surjective f, Mathlib/Logic/Function/Defs.lean:82 -- exact match

legitimate shared decl: Q4907197 'Bijection, injection and surjection' is a single survey article covering all three related function properties, centrally including bijection -- legitimate multi-to-multi with Q180907, not a field/object conflation (no discipline class on either QID)

legitimate: Q729471 'Cantor's diagonal argument' (P31 Q65943 theorem/proof-technique) is exactly theorem cantor_surjective {α} (f : α → Set α) : ¬Surjective f, Mathlib/Logic/Function/Basic.lean:370, docstring literally reads '**Cantor's diagonal argument** implies that there are no surjective functions from α to Set α' -- exact match

reason: overclaim: Q4344872 'Paradoxes of set theory' (P279 Q483372, Wikidata description 'discussion of paradoxes of set theory') is a broad survey/discussion article covering many set-theoretic paradoxes (Russell's paradox, Cantor's paradox, Burali-Forti, etc.), not specifically Cantor's diagonal argument -- cantor_surjective is one narrow lemma, far too specific a decl to stand for the whole survey topic. Downgrade from whatever exact/high grading exists to generalization (the theorem is one instance of the broader paradox literature), matching the pattern of not letting a narrow lemma claim a broad survey-article QID.

field/object conflation: Q4055684 'first-order logic' (P279 Q35148, a formal-systems/field article covering the whole subject: syntax, semantics, completeness, compactness, etc.) is the FIELD; structure Language (Mathlib/ModelTheory/Basic.lean:58, docstring 'defines a language as a pair of functions from the natural numbers to Type l... one sends n to the type of n-ary functions, the other to n-ary relations') is one narrow structural definition used to build up the field, not the field itself. Downgrade to invocation, matching the Q3968/Algebra pattern already in grounding_overrides.jsonl.

field/object conflation, more severe than Q4055684: Q8078 'Logic' (P31 Q15831325/Q816264/Q22811234/Q138589449, Wikidata description 'study of correct reasoning') is a top-level academic discipline spanning philosophical, mathematical, and formal logic broadly -- FirstOrder.Language is a narrow model-theory definitional structure (pair of arity functions for function/relation symbols), nowhere close to the whole discipline of Logic. Downgrade to invocation; this is a worse overclaim than the already-downgraded Q4055684 sibling in this same group.

note: legitimate: Q1851710 'Structure (model theory)' (P31 Q111972893) is exactly class Structure where funMap ... RelMap ..., Mathlib/ModelTheory/Basic.lean:159, docstring 'A first-order structure on a type M consists of interpretations of all the symbols in a given language' -- exact match

legitimate shared decl: Q523607 'interpretation' (P279 Q11348, description 'assignment of meaning to the symbols of a formal language') is the general logic concept that FirstOrder.Language.Structure formalizes for first-order languages specifically -- a Structure literally IS an interpretation (funMap/RelMap assign meaning to function/relation symbols); legitimate related/near-synonym pairing, not a field/object conflation (Q523607 is a term-concept, not a discipline -- no field-of-study class)

reason: field/object conflation, same pattern as Q3968/Algebra: Q173183 'Boolean algebra' is P31 Q1936384 (field of mathematics) and P279 Q3968 (subclass of Algebra, the discipline), Wikidata description 'branch of algebra abstracting logical operations' -- this is the BRANCH/FIELD of study, distinct from Q4973304 'Boolean algebra (structure)' which is the exact algebraic object class BooleanAlgebra formalizes. Downgrade Q173183 to invocation; leave Q4973304 as the exact match.

note: legitimate: Q4973304 'Boolean algebra (structure)' (P279 includes Q1617044 HeytingAlgebra-related classes, description 'lattice that models the classical propositional logic') is exactly class BooleanAlgebra (α : Type u) extends ..., Mathlib/Order/BooleanAlgebra/Defs.lean:103, docstring 'A Boolean algebra is a bounded distributive lattice with a complement operator...' -- exact match, correctly disambiguated from the field-level Q173183 sibling

legitimate: Q4720939 'closure' (description 'the smallest superset of a given set that is closed under a given operation') is exactly what structure ClosureOperator [Preorder α] extends α →o α, Mathlib/Order/Closure.lean:60 (docstring 'a monotone function which is extensive... and idempotent') formalizes in its general order-theoretic form -- exact match

legitimate related pairing, already appropriately hedged (status:partial in source): Q643669 'Generator (mathematics)' (description 'element of a generating set, a subset of an algebraic structure that allows specifying all elements') is the Galois-connection dual of closure operators (e.g. Subgroup.closure is literally the ClosureOperator whose closed elements are the subgroups generated by a set; ClosureOperator.lean docstring even cross-references GaloisConnection/LowerAdjoint). No dedicated 'Generator' decl exists in Mathlib to reassign to (checked: only unrelated hits like NameGenerator, monoidHomMulEquivRootsOfUnityOfGenerator). Leave as-is at existing (already weak) grading -- not a field/object conflation, no discipline class on Q643669.

legitimate: Q2362924 'complete lattice' (description 'partially ordered set in which all subsets have both a supremum and infimum') is exactly class CompleteLattice (α : Type*) extends Lattice α, CompleteSemilatticeSup α, ..., Mathlib/Order/CompleteLattice/Defs.lean:137 -- exact match

legitimate: Q5156540 'completeness (order theory)' (description 'completeness properties assert the existence of certain infima or suprema of a given poset') is the general property CompleteLattice bundles as a structure -- Mathlib has no separate 'completeness property' decl distinct from the CompleteLattice class itself (checked Mathlib/Order/*Complete*: all are specific structures like CompletePartialOrder, CompleteBooleanAlgebra, none a bare 'completeness' predicate), so this is the closest available object -- legitimate, not a field/object conflation (Q5156540 has no discipline class)

reason: field/object conflation: Q5570875 'global optimization' (P279 Q141495, description 'branch of mathematics') is a subfield/discipline, not an object -- def IsMinOn := IsMinFilter f (𝓟 s) a, Mathlib/Order/Filter/Extr.lean:110 ('IsMinOn f s a means f a ≤ f x for all x ∈ s') is a narrow pointwise local/global-minimum-on-a-set predicate, not a formalization of the field of global optimization. Downgrade to invocation.

field/object conflation, same group as Q5570875: Q141495 'mathematical optimization' (P31 Q11862829 academic discipline, description 'study of mathematical algorithms for optimization problems') is the parent field of Q5570875 -- IsMinOn is a single narrow definition (f attains its min on a set), not the discipline. No good folder-level container exists either (checked: Mathlib/Combinatorics/Optimization is combinatorics-specific, not a general optimization-theory home). Downgrade to invocation.

note: legitimate: Q1617044 'Heyting algebra' (description 'bounded lattice that models intuitionistic propositional logic') is exactly class HeytingAlgebra (α : Type*) extends GeneralizedHeytingAlgebra α, OrderBot α, Compl α, Mathlib/Order/Heyting/Basic.lean:154 -- exact match

reason: logic-system vs its algebraic-semantics conflation: Q176786 'intuitionistic logic' (P31 Q12812139, description 'various systems of symbolic logic') is the LOGICAL SYSTEM (proof rules, syntax); HeytingAlgebra is its algebraic/semantic model (Heyting algebras are to intuitionistic logic what Boolean algebras are to classical logic), a well-known but distinct correspondence -- not identical objects. Mathlib actually has a more direct decl for the logic itself: the itauto decision procedure for intuitionistic propositional logic (Mathlib/Tactic/ITauto.lean, 'A decision procedure for intuitionistic propositional logic'), though it's a tactic rather than a typically-tagged definition. Downgrade HeytingAlgebra's match to related rather than exact/invocation -- it models but does not equal intuitionistic logic, and unlike the Q173183/Algebra or Q141495/optimization cases this is not a bare discipline-vs-object mismatch (Q176786 has no field-of-study P31/P279 class), so 'related' (not 'invocation') best captures the semantics-of relationship.
